questions.q: A block of mass 1 kg is resting on a rough horizontal surface with coefficient of friction 0.2 . If an impulse $(10 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-10 \hat{\mathrm{j}}) \mathrm{N}-\mathrm{s}$ is given to block then velocity of block just after the given impulse is
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A person throws a ball $(A)$ vertically upwards with $40 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{sec}$. After 4 sec , he throws another identical ball (B) upwards with same speed. If the collision between balls is head-on perfectly elastic, the speed of ball (A) just after collision is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & v^2=u^2+2 \mathrm{gh} \Rightarrow 0^2=\left(40^2\right)-2(10)(\mathrm{h}) \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{h}=80 \mathrm{~m}\end{aligned}$ where h is maximum height reached by ball $A$ $v=u-g t \Rightarrow t=4 \mathrm{sec}$ $t^{\prime}=\frac{80}{u_A+u_B}=2 \mathrm{sec}$ $\mathrm{u}_{\mathrm{A}}^{\prime}=\mathrm{gt} \mathrm{t}^{\prime}=20 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ $\mathrm{u}_{\mathrm{B}}^{\prime}=40-2 \times 10=20 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ Thus the speed of each ball gets interchanged (for equal mass & elastic collision)
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two identical rods each of mass m and length $\ell$ are placed on a smooth horizontal surface. In case (1) a particle of mass m moving with $V_0$ strikes the rod perpendicularly at its centre. In case (ii), another particle of mass m moving with $\mathrm{V}_0$ strikes the rod perpendicularly at $\ell / 4$ from the centre. In both the cases, particles come to rest just after collision. The ratio of velocity of center of mass of rod in case (i) to that in case (ii) is
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A small sphere and a big sphere are released from rest with a very small gap from height $h$ as shown in the figure. The mass of bigger sphere is very large as compared to mass of smaller sphere the height from the point of collision of smaller sphere with the bigger sphere to which the smaller sphere will rise if all the collisions are elastic
questions.solution:
conservation of momentum
$M \sqrt{2 g h}-m \sqrt{2 g h}=M V_1+m V_2$     (1)
$-1=\frac{V_1-V_2}{2 \sqrt{2 g h}}$      (2)
$V_2=3 \sqrt{2 g h}$
$h^{\prime}=\frac{V_2^2}{2 g}=9 h$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Choose the correct statement about centre of mass.
options: For calculation of any quantity associated with extended body we can assume total mass of body to be concentrated at centre of mass of the body.
Centre of mass of a body moves in the same way as if all external forces are acting on it and total mass of body is concentrated at it.
Torque of centrifugal force about centre of mass on rigid body undergoing rotational motion is always zero.
questions.solution: We can not assume total mass to be concentrated at centre of mass for calculation of all the thing e.g. for calculation of moment of inertia, calculation of torque of centrifugal force on a rod about hinge as shown.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A mass $m$ is attached to one end of a light rod. This assembly is placed on the origin as shown in the figure. If all surfaces are smooth then the position of lower end when the rod becomes horizontal after its upper end has been disturbed gently towards right is
questions.solution:
As there does not exist any external horizontal force so $X$ - coordinate of centre of mass will not charge
$\Rightarrow$ position of lower end is $(-\ell, 0)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
